title: Thai Butternut Squash Soup

overview.description: A smooth, warming Thai-inspired soup of roasted butternut squash, homemade red curry paste, coconut milk and chicken stock.

ingredients: 2 tbsp homemade Thai red curry paste
method: Roast the butternut squash at 200°C (180°C fan) for 30 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised.
Meanwhile, heat the oil over a medium heat.
Cook the onion for 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the homemade red curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock and coconut milk.
Bring to a gentle simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with the fish sauce and lime juice.

learning.did-you-know: Thai soups are built around balancing salty, sour, sweet and spicy flavours rather than allowing one flavour to dominate.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How roasting vegetables develops sweetness and how Thai curry paste can be used as the flavour base for soups.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A simple freezer-friendly soup that makes excellent use of homemade curry paste, chicken stock and a vegetable that's already a regular in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Roasting the squash gives a much deeper flavour than boiling it.
Homemade Thai red curry paste really shines in this recipe.
Season with fish sauce and lime at the end rather than salt first.
Blend until silky smooth for the best texture.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently without boiling to keep the coconut milk smooth.
